name: Mount Tambora
eruptionHistory: The 1815 eruption was the largest in recorded history (VEI 7). It killed over 71,000 people and caused the 'Year Without a Summer' in 1816 with global crop failures.
description: Mount Tambora's 1815 eruption was the most powerful volcanic eruption in recorded human history. It reduced the mountain's height from ~4,300m to 2,850m and created a 6km-wide caldera.
